titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Dorothy Alexander Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Nora Soto

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday July 1, 2023 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of black-and-white and color photographic prints, contact sheets, negatives, slides, indices and other papers, audiovisual media, and digital files related to the work of Canadian-American photographer Dorothy Alexander. Photographic materials encompass her professional freelance work and personal creative endeavors, particularly street photography.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 52 (film reels): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 53 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Indices, 2016. II. Freelance Photography, 1960s-2010. III. Street Photography, 1981-2007. IV. Papers, 1979-2002. V. Audiovisual Media, undated. VI. Computer Files.

bioghist.head: Dorothy Alexander (1934?-)
bioghist.p: Dorothy Alexander is a Canadian-American photographer and architect. Born Dorothy Baxter in Saint Lambert, Quebec, Canada, she studied architecture at McGill University before immigrating to the United States. In 1961, she obtained her license to practice architecture in California. She worked as an architect for John Carl Warnecke and the firm Wurster, Bernardi, and Emmons in San Francisco, and later established her own firm under the name D. B. Alexander. She moved to New York in 1967.

Upon moving to New York, she began taking street portraits of New Yorkers with an old Zeiss Ikon Super Ikonta camera. With no formal training, she subsequently transitioned to a career in photography. From 1979 to 2008, she worked as freelance photographer for the American Academy of Arts and Letters. Other clients included the Academy of American Poets, The Writer's Voice at the West Side YMCA, and The Globe and Mail . Alexander also continued to work in the field of architecture on assignments for professional organizations like the Architectural League of New York and publications such as Architectural Digest . In the 1980s, she worked as staff photographer for Skyline , published by the Institute for Architecture and Urban Studies, and Oculus , the quarterly magazine of the American Institute of Architects New York chapter.

Alexander's photography is featured in the collections of the Bibliothèque Nationale de France and the Smithsonian Institution's National Portrait Gallery, as well as the private collection of Dorothy and Herbert Vogel. In the 1990s, her street portraits were exhibited in the show "New York Women" at the Donnell Library Center.
Alexander lives in New York City. She was previously married to architect William Alexander.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Dorothy Alexander Photographs. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of black-and-white and color photographic prints, contact sheets, negatives, slides, indices and other papers, audiovisual media, and digital files related to the work of Canadian-American photographer Dorothy Alexander. Photographic materials encompass her professional freelance work and personal creative endeavors, particularly street photography.

The collection contains Alexander's photographs of events and projects for organizations such as the Academy of American Poets, the American Academy of Arts and Letters, the American Institute of Architects, the Architectural League of New York, the Institute for Architecture and Urban Studies, the Municipal Art Society of New York, the New York Institute for the Humanities, and the Writer's Voice at the West Side YMCA. Subjects of those photographs include Peter Eisenman, Michael Graves, Robert Stern, and Herbert and Dorothy Vogel. Alexander's street photography features images of women and urban landscapes. Many items throughout the collection are inscribed with dates, names of events or subjects, or project numbers assigned by Alexander.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Dorothy Alexander Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: American Academy of Arts and Letters
Institute for Architecture and Urban Studies
Municipal Art Society of New York
New York Institute for the Humanities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alexander assigned project numbers to the majority of her photographic material, including both professional assignments and personal work. Numbers range from two to six digits, which sometimes identify the year and month of image creation, or the film roll and exposure frame numbers. For most of the photographic material in the collection, project numbers are inscribed on the verso of prints, on enclosures, or on mounts. These materials are present in Series II. Freelance Photography, and Series III. Street Photography.

This series consists of photocopied indices documenting project numbers, dates, and subjects of Alexander's early work assignments from the 1970s and 1980s, and freelance assignments from 1991 to 2009. There are also four photocopied "shoot books" accompanying the 1991-2009 index.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series documents Alexander's freelance work, largely comprised of event photography and editorial photography related to the fields of architecture, poetry, and literature. Alexander's clients include New York-based cultural organizations such as the Academy of American Poets, the American Academy of Arts and Letters, the American Institute of Architects, the Architectural League of New York, the Institute for Architecture and Urban Studies, the Municipal Art Society of New York, the New York Institute for the Humanities, and the Writer's Voice at the West Side YMCA. The series contains images of architects Peter Eisenman, Michael Graves, and Robert Stern, among others, at parties, ceremonials, panels, and other events. There are also assignments for publications such as Architectural Record and Interior Design , as well as portraits, and photographs for a project on New York art collectors Herbert and Dorothy Vogel. Materials consist of contact sheets, negatives, and a small amount of photographic prints.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Chronological Files, Assignments by Client, and Assignments by Project Number.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily professional photography, although a small amount of personal photographs is present.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in chronological order. In some cases, groups of materials are sub-arranged alphabetically by file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The subseries contains contact sheets and negatives filed by project number, which correspond to the indices in Series I.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Contact Sheets and Negatives, and Negatives. Each is arranged by project number as assigned by Alexander, with certain files spanning a wide range of numbers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of color street portraits, primarily of women in New York City, as well as urban landscapes, and personal travel photography taken with a variety of camera models such as the Diana, Hasselblad, Holga, Rolleiflex, and Super Ikonta. In addition to photographic prints, contact sheets, and negatives, the series also includes a small amount of slides.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Binders, and Proof Prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of contact sheets and negatives previously housed in binders. Materials were removed from the binders and rehoused in folders during processing. Files contain a mix of items with project numbers and unnumbered items, reflecting Alexander's original arrangement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files contain square color prints taken with the Super Ikonta. Several sets of photographs are accompanied by negatives.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of Alexander's resumes, materials relating to Alexander's exhibitions at Gotham Book Mart, the Donnell Library Center, and other galleries, and various loose photographs.

Portraits of Writers and Poets , Gotham Book Mart exhibition

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 52 (film reels): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: External hard drive with autograph note, signed

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The files on this external hard drive were transferred directly into the Library's digital preservation system. The numbered headings in the finding aid replicate the names and contents of Dorothy Alexander's top-level folders on the drive.

odd.head: General
odd.p: The text of the note Dorothy Alexander wrote to accompany the external hard drive reads: "Aug 23, 2016 NOTE: THIS DRIVE HAS BEEN REFORMATTED FOR TRANSFER OF FILES FROM MACBOOK PRO LAPTOP OSX EL CAPITAN. IT CONTAINS SOME UNIVERSAL IMAGE FILES, JPEG AND TIFF; SOME FILES PROPRIETARY TO ADOBE PHOTOSHOP (VERSION 6) AND RAW FILES PROPRIETARY TO CAMERA(S) THAT CAPTURED THEM! DA"
